title: Parallel batch: bundle work into BatchHeader tasks instead of one long-running job
instruction: In the sandbox model, split a long job into parallel batch tasks. Ground it first with get_knowledge(topic="parallel-batch"). Create: (1) a SysOperation service class DemoParallelWorker with a `public void processBundle(DemoParallelContract _contract)` marked [SysEntryPointAttribute] that processes exactly the bundle identified by the contract; (2) a data contract class DemoParallelContract with [DataContractAttribute] and [DataMemberAttribute] parm methods for the bundle bounds (from/to key) - the contract must be serializable, so no List/Map members; (3) a class DemoParallelScheduler with `public static void scheduleAll()` that partitions the ConDemoNoteHeader rows into bundles, and for each bundle creates a task on a BatchHeader (BatchHeader::construct(), addRuntimeTask(...) with a SysOperationServiceController targeting the worker, then save()), so the tasks run in parallel. Doing the work inline in a single loop FAILS this case. Confirm the BatchHeader member signatures with search/get_method before writing them - do not guess. All objects must build with zero BP errors.
